# t18.1 - Security audit rerun after remediation

## Verdict

PASS with residual MEDIUM risk. The t18 remediation closed the prior CRITICAL and HIGH findings around anonymous admin/runtime mutation, unauthenticated REST and streaming access, and plaintext secret exposure. The remaining gap is dependency assurance: the runtime still resolves Derby 10.14.2.0, which is below Hibernate's supported minimum, and the rerun did not produce a usable OWASP Dependency-Check report to establish a clean CVE verdict.

## Scope

- Re-audited the remediated auth/session boundary for `/config`, `/rest/quotes`, `/rest/broadcastevents`, and `/marketsummary`.
- Re-audited password hashing and credit-card redaction on the active Spring Boot auth path.
- Re-checked the resolved dependency set and available dependency-scan evidence.

## Closed Findings

### CLOSED - `/config`, REST, SSE, and WebSocket traffic now sit behind explicit session guards

- Evidence:
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\WebAccessConfiguration.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\WebAccessConfiguration.java#L19) places `/config` behind an operator requirement.
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\WebAccessConfiguration.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\WebAccessConfiguration.java#L21) places `/rest/quotes/**` and `/rest/broadcastevents` behind an authenticated-session requirement.
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\CompatibilitySessionAccessInterceptor.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\CompatibilitySessionAccessInterceptor.java#L22) through [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\CompatibilitySessionAccessInterceptor.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\CompatibilitySessionAccessInterceptor.java#L29) reject anonymous requests with `401` and non-operator `/config` access with `403`.
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\MarketSummaryHandshakeInterceptor.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\MarketSummaryHandshakeInterceptor.java#L26) through [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\MarketSummaryHandshakeInterceptor.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\web\mvc\MarketSummaryHandshakeInterceptor.java#L42) reject anonymous and cross-origin WebSocket handshakes.
- Validation:
  - `TradeConfigControllerTest`, `RestAccessBoundaryTest`, and `MarketSummaryHandshakeInterceptorTest` all passed in the focused rerun.

### CLOSED - Active Spring Boot auth path now hashes passwords and redacts credit-card values

- Evidence:
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\pom.xml](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\pom.xml#L69) declares `spring-security-crypto`.
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\SecurityCryptoConfiguration.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\SecurityCryptoConfiguration.java#L13) supplies a `BCryptPasswordEncoder`.
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java#L52) and [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java#L67) hash updated and legacy-login-upgraded passwords.
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java#L54), [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java#L98), and [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\application\auth\AuthenticationApplicationService.java#L133) mask credit-card values before persistence.
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\entities\AccountProfileDataBean.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\entities\AccountProfileDataBean.java#L84) through [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\entities\AccountProfileDataBean.java](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\src\main\java\com\ibm\websphere\samples\daytrader\entities\AccountProfileDataBean.java#L91) render `[PROTECTED]` for passwords and masked credit-card output.
- Validation:
  - `AuthenticationApplicationServiceTest` and `AccountProfileDataBeanTest` both passed in the focused rerun.

## Remaining Finding

### MEDIUM - Dependency assurance remains incomplete and the runtime still pins unsupported Derby 10.14.2.0

- Severity: MEDIUM
- Owner: backend
- Evidence:
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\pom.xml](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\pom.xml#L18) pins `version.derby` to `10.14.2.0` and [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\pom.xml](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\pom.xml#L97) uses that property for the runtime dependency.
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\target\dependency-tree.txt](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\target\dependency-tree.txt#L81) resolves `org.apache.derby:derby:10.14.2.0` on the active classpath.
  - [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\target\surefire-reports\TEST-com.ibm.websphere.samples.daytrader.integration.journeys.DayTraderJourneyIntegrationTest.xml](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\target\surefire-reports\TEST-com.ibm.websphere.samples.daytrader.integration.journeys.DayTraderJourneyIntegrationTest.xml#L90) records Hibernate warning `HHH000511`, stating Derby 10.14 is below the supported minimum 10.15.2.
  - The OWASP Dependency-Check rerun did not leave any files under `target/dependency-check`, so this task still lacks a usable CVE-backed report.
- Impact: The authentication and secret-handling regressions are fixed, but the project still cannot claim a clean dependency posture. Running below the supported Derby baseline leaves dependency risk triage incomplete.
- Required fix: Upgrade Derby to a Hibernate-supported version, then rerun OWASP Dependency-Check with a working NVD-backed configuration and archive the generated report.

## Validation Evidence

- Focused regression command: `cmd /d /c "set JAVA_HOME=%USERPROFILE%\scoop\apps\microsoft17-jdk\current&& set PATH=%JAVA_HOME%\bin;%PATH%&& mvn -Dtest=TradeConfigControllerTest,RestAccessBoundaryTest,MarketSummaryHandshakeInterceptorTest,AuthenticationApplicationServiceTest,AccountProfileDataBeanTest,MarketSummaryPublisherTest test"`
- Result: `BUILD SUCCESS`
- Dependency tree evidence: existing resolved tree at [c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\target\dependency-tree.txt](c:\Users\jiangma\Workspace\repos\sample.daytrader8\target\dependency-tree.txt#L81)
- Dependency-check evidence: no generated files found under `target/dependency-check` after the rerun attempt

## Downstream Impact

- `t20` can proceed on the remediated auth/session boundary and secret-handling surface.
- Release or conformance sign-off should keep a dependency-risk note open until Derby is upgraded and a usable OWASP report is captured.
